session.user:
# /erk:plan-implement
Implement a plan - either from GitHub, a markdown file, an existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/`, or by saving the current plan first.
This is the primary implementation workflow - it orchestrates:
1. Setting up the impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` (from issue, file, existing folder, or fresh plan) 2. Executing the implementation 3. Running CI and submitting the PR
## Prerequisites
- Must be in a git repository managed by erk - GitHub CLI (`gh`) must be authenticated - One of: - A plan number, URL, or file path argument - An existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - A plan branch checked out (e.g., `plnd/...` or `P{number}-...`) - A plan in `~/.claude/plans/` (from plan mode)
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-implement # Use impl directory, detect from branch, or save current plan /erk:plan-implement 2521 # Fetch and implement plan #2521 /erk:plan-implement https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 # URL form /erk:plan-implement ./my-plan.md # Implement from local markdown file ```
---
## CRITICAL: PR Operations
**NEVER** run direct GitHub CLI commands for PR operations during implementation:
- `gh pr edit` — destroys plan-header metadata block - `gh pr ready` — bypasses lifecycle tracking - `gh pr close` / `gh pr merge`
All PR operations are handled by `erk pr submit` in Step 13. It automatically:
- Generates the PR title and body - Preserves the plan-header metadata block - Marks draft PRs as ready for review - Updates lifecycle stage
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Set Up Implementation
Parse `` and run the consolidated setup command:
- **If numeric** (e.g., `2521`): `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/.../issues/2521`): Extract number, `erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521` - **If path to file** (anything else non-empty): `erk exec setup-impl --file <path>` - **If empty**: `erk exec setup-impl` (auto-detects from `.erk/impl-context/`, branch, or fails)
```bash erk exec setup-impl [--issue <N> | --file <path>] ```
This single command handles:
- Fetching plan from GitHub (draft PR) - Setting up from a local markdown file - Auto-detecting from existing impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/` - Auto-detecting plan number from branch name (P{number}-... or PR lookup) - Creating/checking out the feature branch - Creating impl directory under `.erk/impl-context/<branch>/` with plan content - Running impl-init validation - Cleaning up `.erk/impl-context/` staging directory (git rm + commit + push)
Otherwise, generate a branch slug and set up from the specified issue:
1. Fetch the title: `gh pr view <ISSUE_ARG> --json title -q .title` 2. Generate a branch slug from the title: - 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc" 3. Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <ISSUE_ARG> --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
If `setup-impl` exits with code 1 and `error: "no_plan_found"`, fall back to saving the current plan:
Generate a branch slug from the plan title (the first `# ` heading in the plan file):
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Store as `BRANCH_SLUG`
Save the current plan to GitHub and capture the plan number:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="cb6da725-616e-4577-ad61-abad1c404042" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" ```
Parse the JSON output to get `plan_number`, then:
```bash erk exec setup-impl --issue <plan_number> ```
The `setup-impl` output includes `related_docs` (skills and docs to load) and `has_plan_tracking` (whether GitHub issue tracking is active).
If this fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 2: Read Plan and Load Context
Read `plan.md` from the impl directory set up in Step 1 to understand:
- Overall goal and context - Context & Understanding sections (API quirks, architectural insights, pitfalls) - Implementation phases and dependencies - Success criteria
**Context Consumption**: Plans contain expensive discoveries. Ignoring `[CRITICAL:]` tags, "Related Context:" subsections, or "DO NOT" items causes repeated mistakes.
### Step 3: Load Related Documentation
If plan contains "Related Documentation" section, load listed skills via Skill tool and read listed docs.
### Step 4: Create TodoWrite Entries
Create todo entries for each phase from the plan.
### Step 5: Signal GitHub Started
```bash erk exec impl-signal started --session-id="cb6da725-616e-4577-ad61-abad1c404042" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This also deletes the Claude plan file (from `~/.claude/plans/`) since:
- The content has been saved to GitHub - The content has been snapshotted to `.erk/scratch/` - Keeping it could cause confusion if the user tries to re-save
### Step 6: Execute Each Phase Sequentially
For each phase:
1. **Mark phase as in_progress** (in TodoWrite) 2. **Read task requirements** carefully 3. **Implement code AND tests together**: - Load `dignified-python-313` skill for coding standards - Load `fake-driven-testing` skill for test patterns - Follow project AGENTS.md standards 4. **Mark phase as completed** (in TodoWrite) 5. **Report progress**: changes made, what's next
**Important:** `plan.md` is immutable - NEVER edit during implementation
### Step 7: Report Progress
After each phase: report changes made and what's next.
### Step 8: Final Verification
Confirm all tasks executed, success criteria met, note deviations, summarize changes.
### Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended
```bash erk exec impl-signal ended --session-id="cb6da725-616e-4577-ad61-abad1c404042" 2>/dev/null || true ```
### Step 10: Upload Session for Async Learn
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id="cb6da725-616e-4577-ad61-abad1c404042" 2>/dev/null || true ```
This reads plan reference from the impl directory, captures session info, and uploads for async learn processing.
### Step 11: Clean Up Impl Context
Remove the `.erk/impl-context/` directory from the repo:
```bash erk exec cleanup-impl-context ```
This removes `.erk/impl-context/`, stages the deletion, commits, and pushes — all idempotent.
### Step 12: Run CI Iteratively
1. If `.erk/prompt-hooks/post-plan-implement-ci.md` exists: follow its instructions 2. Otherwise: check CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md for CI commands
### Step 13: Submit PR
Push the branch and create or update the PR using the Graphite-aware submit pipeline:
```bash erk pr submit ```
This handles pushing commits, creating/updating the PR, generating the PR description, and enhancing with Graphite stack metadata when available.
After successful submission, signal lifecycle transition:
```bash erk exec impl-signal submitted 2>/dev/null || true ```
Then validate PR completion invariants:
```bash erk pr check --stage=impl ```
This validates PR submission readiness including implementation-specific checks (e.g., `.erk/impl-context/` must be cleaned up). If checks fail, display output and warn user.
### Step 14: Output Format
- **Start**: "Setting up implementation..." or "Fetching plan #X..." - **After setup**: "Implementation environment ready, reading plan..." - **Each phase**: "Phase X: [brief description]" with code changes - **End**: "Plan execution complete. [Summary]"
---
## Related Commands
- `/erk:plan-save` - Save plan only, don't implement (for defer-to-later workflow) - `/erk:replan` - Re-plan an existing plan with current codebase state
session.assistant: Setting up implementation...

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Auto-detect and set up implementation from current branch

session.assistant: Implementation environment ready. Reading plan...
session.tool_result:
1→# Fix Graphite divergence in incremental-dispatch after plumbing commit 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→`erk exec incremental-dispatch` uses `commit_files_to_branch` (git plumbing) to commit plan files to a branch without checkout. This advances the branch ref but bypasses Graphite, causing Graphite to report divergence. The fix already exists in `plan_save.py` (line 259-260) but was never applied to `incremental_dispatch.py`. 6→ 7→Secondary issue: when the branch is checked out in the current worktree, the index sync uses `git checkout HEAD -- <files>` which is fragile. `plan_save.py` uses the better pattern of writing files to disk + `stage_files(force=True)`. 8→ 9→## Changes 10→ 11→### File: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py` 12→ 13→**1. Add Graphite retrack after plumbing commit (after line 132)** 14→ 15→After `commit_files_to_branch`, add: 16→ 17→```python 18→# Update Graphite tracking after plumbing commit advanced the branch 19→erk_ctx.branch_manager.retrack_branch(repo_root, branch_name) 20→``` 21→ 22→**2. Replace index sync block (lines 134-144)** 23→ 24→Replace the `git checkout HEAD -- <files>` approach with `plan_save.py`'s pattern: 25→ 26→```python 27→if checked_out_path is not None: 28→ for rel_path, content in files.items(): 29→ abs_path = checked_out_path / rel_path 30→ abs_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
... [17 lines omitted] ...
35→This also removes the `run_subprocess_with_context` import (only used by the old index sync) and the `try/except` that silently swallowed sync failures. 46→2. Manual: `erk exec incremental-dispatch --plan-file <f> --pr <n>` from a worktree where the branch is checked out, then `gt log` — no divergence warning
session.assistant: Now let me read the target file and the reference in plan_save.py.

Unlike regular dispatch, this does not require 8→the erk-plan label — just an OPEN PR. 9→""" 10→ 11→import json 12→import logging 13→from pathlib import Path 14→ 15→import click 16→ 17→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_cmd import load_workflow_config 18→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced 19→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 20→from erk.cli.constants import DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME 21→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 22→ require_context, 23→ require_git, 24→ require_github, 25→ require_repo_root, 26→ require_time, 27→) 28→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 29→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 30→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import (
... [186 lines omitted] ...

245→ if xml_file.is_file(): 246→ files[f"{IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR}/sessions/{xml_file.name}"] = xml_file.read_text( 247→ encoding="utf-8" 248→ ) 249→ 250→ git.commit.commit_files_to_branch( 251→ repo_root, 252→ branch=branch_name, 253→ files=files, 254→ message=f"Add plan: {title}", 255→ ) 256→ 257→ # Update Graphite tracking after plumbing commit advanced the branch 258→ # (create_branch tracked position A, but commit_files_to_branch moved to B) 259→ if not current_branch_flag: 260→ branch_manager.retrack_branch(repo_root, branch_name) 261→ 262→ # When saving on the current branch, the git plumbing commit advanced HEAD 263→ # but left the working tree and index unchanged. Write files to disk and 264→ # stage them so git status is clean. 265→ if current_branch_flag: 266→ for rel_path, content in files.items(): 267→ abs_path = repo_root / rel_path 268→ abs_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) 269→ abs_path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8") 270→ git.commit.stage_files(repo_root, list(files.keys()), force=True) 271→ 272→ git.remote.push_to_remote(cwd, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=True, force=False) 273→ 274→ # Build metadata — base_ref_name sets the PR base ref

tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py:385: # Verify retrack_branch was called with repo root and branch name tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py:386: assert len(graphite.retrack_branch_calls) == 1 tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py:387: retrack_cwd, retrack_branch = graphite.retrack_branch_calls[0] tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py:389: assert retrack_branch == "feature" tests/unit/branch_manager/test_graphite_branch_manager.py:225: # Parent branch should have been retracked tests/unit/branch_manager/test_graphite_branch_manager.py:227: branch == "parent-branch" for (_, branch) in fake_graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch_calls tests/unit/branch_manager/test_graphite_branch_manager.py:325: # Parent should NOT have been retracked (no divergence) tests/unit/branch_manager/test_graphite_branch_manager.py:326: assert len(fake_graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch_calls) == 0 tests/commands/pr/test_checkout_graphite_linking.py:284: # retrack_branch must be called with repo root, not worktree path tests/commands/pr/test_checkout_graphite_linking.py:285: assert len(graphite.retrack_branch_calls) == 1

session.assistant: Let me check how the branch_manager's retrack is tracked in tests - it goes through the branch manager, not directly through graphite in this case.

The existing `test_incremental_dispatch_checked_out_branch` test already covers the checked-out path. Let me verify that the `stage_files` call is tracked by FakeGit's commit object so we can assert on it.
session.tool_result: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:223: ctx.git.commit.stage_files(state.repo_root, [".erk/impl-context/"], force=True) src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py:270: git.commit.stage_files(repo_root, list(files.keys()), force=True) src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/cleanup_impl_context.py:61: git.commit.stage_files(repo_root, [".erk/impl-context/"], force=True) src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py:141: git.commit.stage_files(checked_out_path, list(files.keys()), force=True)
session.assistant: The tests pass. The changes are correct — `stage_files` is on the `git.commit` gateway which is already faked. The existing tests pass because the fakes handle these method calls.
Now let me signal started and proceed with CI.
Implementation complete. Let me signal ended and run CI.
Now let me run CI.
session.user:
You are an implementation finalizer for rapid Python-focused development feedback. Your task is to run `make py-fast-ci` and iteratively fix any issues until all CI checks pass successfully.
## Your Mission
Run the Python-only fast CI pipeline (`make py-fast-ci`) from the repository root and automatically fix any failures. Keep iterating until all checks pass or you get stuck on an issue that requires human intervention.
**py-fast-ci vs fast-ci**: The `py-fast-ci` target runs Python checks (lint, format, ty, pytest), skipping markdown and documentation checks. Use this when you're iterating on Python code and don't want to wait for prettier/markdown validation. Use `/fast-ci` when you need the full fast CI pipeline including all checks.
## CI Pipeline (make py-fast-ci)
The `make py-fast-ci` target runs these checks in order:
1. **lint** - Ruff linting checks 2. **format** - Python formatting check (ruff format --check) 3. **ty** - Type checking 4. **test-unit-erk** - Pytest unit tests for erk 5. **test-erk-dev** - Pytest tests for erk-dev
**Skipped** (compared to fast-ci):
- Prettier check (markdown formatting) - Markdown check (AGENTS.md compliance) - Docs validate/sync
## Iteration Process
Load the `ci-iteration` skill for the iterative fix workflow.
## Fail-Fast Workflow
This command uses a two-phase approach to fail fast on Python code quality issues:
1. **Phase 1 (Fail-Fast)**: Run `make lint format ty` together (ruff lint + ruff format + type checking). If ANY fails, stop immediately to fix issues before proceeding to test runs. 2. **Phase 2 (Test Runs)**: Only after phase 1 passes, run `make test-unit-erk test-erk-dev`.
This ensures that syntax/formatting and type errors are caught and fixed first, providing rapid feedback without waiting for test execution.
## Begin Now
Start by using the Task tool with the devrun agent to run the fail-fast phase (`make lint format ty`) from the repository root. If that passes, proceed with test targets (`make test-unit-erk test-erk-dev`). Track your progress with TodoWrite and report your final status clearly.
**Remember**:
- NEVER run pytest/ty/ruff/prettier/make/gt directly via Bash - Always use the Task tool with subagent_type: devrun - Covered tools: pytest, ty, ruff, prettier, make, gt - Always ensure make commands execute from the repository root directory

tool_use.name: TaskCreate
tool_use.param: Run py-fast-ci Phase 1: lint, format, ty
Run make lint format ty to check code quality before tests
Running lint, format, and type checks

session.tool_result: Task #2 created successfully: Run py-fast-ci Phase 1: lint, format, ty

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: Run `make lint format ty` from /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-07 and report all output, including any failures.

session.tool_result:
Summary of results:
**lint** (ruff check): PASSED - All checks passed!
**format-check** (ruff format --check): FAILED - 1 file would be reformatted: `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_setup_impl_from_pr.py` - 1,639 files already formatted - Exit code: 1
**ty** (type checking): Did not run - The make chain stopped at the format-check failure, so ty was not executed.
The primary issue is that the test file `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_setup_impl_from_pr.py` needs reformatting. Run `make fix` or `uv run ruff format tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_setup_impl_from_pr.py` to fix the formatting. agentId: af38cee662081c173 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed) <usage>total_tokens: 17060 tool_uses: 3 duration_ms: 9643</usage>

session.tool_result:
## Results
All checks passed successfully:
- **lint** (ruff check): All checks passed! - **format-check** (ruff format --check): 1640 files already formatted - **ty** (ty check): All checks passed!
There is no `format` target in the Makefile. The available formatting targets are: - `format-check` - Check if files are formatted (used in CI) - `prettier` - Apply prettier formatting to markdown files (writes changes) - `fix` - Apply ruff and prettier fixes in-place agentId: a04eff073a455b9b5 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed) <usage>total_tokens: 17105 tool_uses: 6 duration_ms: 11561</usage>
session.assistant: Phase 1 passes. Now Phase 2 — tests.

session.tool_result:
🚀 Submitting PR...
Resolving branch and plan context... Committing uncommitted changes... Checking for existing PR... Phase 1: Graphite Submit Auto-forcing: plan implementation branch Running gt submit... Running in non-interactive mode. Inline prompts to fill PR fields will be skipped.
🥞 Validating that this Graphite stack is ready to submit...
✏️ Preparing to submit PRs for the following branches... ▸ plnd/consolidate-checkout-prepa-03-06-0452 (Update code) ▸ plnd/fix-incremental-dispatch-r-03-06-1247 (New parent)
🚀 Pushing branches to remote...
📨 Creating/updating PRs... plnd/consolidate-checkout-prepa-03-06-0452: https://app.graphite.com/github/pr/dagster-io/erk/8830 (updated) plnd/fix-incremental-dispatch-r-03-06-1247: https://app.graphite.com/github/pr/dagster-io/erk/8850 (updated) WARNING: Branch plnd/consolidate-checkout-prepa-03-06-0452 has been updated remotely. Force submitting local version to remote... Graphite submit completed
Getting PR info... PR #8850 ready

session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Summary of changes:
**File modified:** `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py`
1. **Added Graphite retrack** after `commit_files_to_branch` — calls `branch_manager.retrack_branch()` to keep Graphite in sync after the plumbing commit advances the branch ref 2. **Replaced fragile index sync** — swapped `git checkout HEAD -- <files>` with the write-to-disk + `stage_files(force=True)` pattern (matching `plan_save.py`) 3. **Cleaned up imports** — removed unused `run_subprocess_with_context`, `logging`, and `logger`
**PR:** https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8850
